title: 10 best herbal tinctures for ringworm

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with ringworm because of its natural antifungal properties, which target the fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The active compounds in wormwood, such as thujone and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi like Trichophyton, Microsporum, and Epidermophyton, which are responsible for ringworm. By using a herbal tincture containing wormwood, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling, and promote healing of the affected skin areas.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Wormwood's antifungal compounds, such as thujone and camphor, help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for ringworm, including Trichophyton rubrum and Microsporum canis.
Anti-inflammatory property: The bioactive compounds in wormwood, like sesquiterpenes, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antimicrobial property: Wormwood's antimicrobial activity helps to combat the spread of fungal pathogens by inhibiting their ability to multiply and produce toxins, thereby reducing symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone, a terpene compound, has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help combat ringworm fungus, thereby promoting healing and reducing symptoms.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate, a sesquiterpene, exhibits antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe ringworm-infected skin, reduce redness, and prevent further infection.

Artemisinin: Artemisinin, a sesquiterpene lactone, possesses antifungal and antioxidant properties that may help eliminate fungal infections, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair in ringworm-affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of fresh or dried artemisia absinthium flowers and leaves and chop them into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped herb with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking it every day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid to small dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antifungal compounds, which help to combat the fungal infection causing ringworm.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium contain essential oils that possess antimicrobial properties, effective against ringworm.
Buds: The buds are known to possess antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to speed up the healing process and eliminate the fungal infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which target the fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The active compounds in pot marigold, such as calendulin and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary bacterial infections, allowing the body to naturally recover from ringworm. As a result, pot marigold tinctures can be a effective natural remedy for treating ringworm and alleviating symptoms such as itching, redness, and scarring.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Pot marigold's tannins and flavonoids exhibit antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens such as Trichophyton rubrum, a common cause of ringworm.
Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tinctures, including luteolin and apigenin, can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm lesions.

Wound-healing promotion: Pot marigold's antioxidants and flavonoids have been shown to enhance collagen synthesis, promote tissue repair, and accelerate wound healing, which can help to improve the appearance of ringworm-affected skin.
remedy_constituents: Calendulin: A triterpene saponin complex found in Calendula officinalis, calendulin helps with ringworm by inhibiting the growth of fungi and bacteria, thus reducing the severity of the infection.

N-alkanoyl-4-hydroxyphenylalanine amide (HPPA): A phenolic compound, HPPA has been shown to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat ringworm by preventing the spread of fungal and bacterial agents.

Isorhapontigenin: A flavonoid with potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, isorhapontigenin helps with ringworm by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of fungal pathogens that cause the infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of calendulin, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They contain a similar profile of bioactive compounds as the flowers, making them a suitable alternative for tincture production.
Roots: They are used in traditional herbal medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate ringworm symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula flowers in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day sets in.
Clean the harvested flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris from the field.
Use a glass jar with a lid to make the tincture with a ratio of 1 part flowers to 2 parts 80 proof vodka.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to aid extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with ringworm because of its natural antifungal properties, which combat fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The essential oils present in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, lemongrass tinctures can help soothe itchy skin and reduce the appearance of lesions, making it a natural and effective treatment option for ringworm sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, bacteria, and viruses, including Candida albicans, which is often responsible for ringworm infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm.

Antifungal properties: The terpenes present in lemongrass, such as limonene and beta-pinene, have been found to exhibit potent antifungal activity against a range of fungi, including those that cause ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: A terpene that exhibits antifungal properties, helping to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm.
Cymbopogonolide: A phenolic compound with reported antifungal and antibacterial activities, which could help combat fungal infections such as ringworm.
Cymbopogonol: Although less studied, it may also possess antifungal properties, contributing to the potential of Cymbopogon citratus tinctures in treating ringworm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain citronella oil, which has antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: They possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can aid in treating ringworm and other fungal infections.
Stems: They contain compounds that exhibit antifungal activity, which can help in managing ringworm symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper bags for 7 to 10 days in a cool area.
Combine 1 part dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 2 parts 80% grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass jar, discard the solids and store the tincture in the refrigerator.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial infections, including ringworm, by attacking and destroying the pathogens.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Kansas coneflower reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a more rapid healing process.

Antifungal properties: Kansas coneflower's antifungal properties specifically target fungi that cause ringworm, such as Trichophyton, Microsporum, and Epidermophyton species, helping to eradicate the infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The tincture's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has antimicrobial properties that help prevent secondary infections, allowing the body to focus on clearing up the ringworm infection. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective alternative for treating ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, which can contribute to the resolution of ringworm symptoms.

Alkaloids (primarily echinulin and echinacoside): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat fungal infections such as ringworm, and also exhibit anti-inflammatory effects to soothe the affected area.

Flavonoids (primarily kaempferol and quercetin): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected skin, promoting healing and reducing the severity of ringworm symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The primary part of Echinacea angustifolia used for medicinal purposes due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Leaves: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Stems: Stems: Employed due to their ability to act as a natural anti-fungal agent and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50-75 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root, harvested from the fall season.
Combine the root with 75% vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and label it, storing it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with ringworm because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the tincture have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, including those responsible for ringworm. Additionally, the tincture's ability to reduce inflammation and itching provides relief from the uncomfortable symptoms associated with ringworm infections. By targeting both the infection and its symptoms, Tasmanian blue gum tinctures offer a natural and effective approach to treating ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture contains antimicrobial compounds like cineole, which help combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of the infecting organism.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of secondary infections.

Antifungal properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antifungal properties can help combat the fungal infection itself, reducing the severity and duration of ringworm symptoms such as rashes, blisters, and lesions.
remedy_constituents: 1,8-Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm.
Catechin: As a phenolic compound, catechin exhibits antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to the treatment of ringworm.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, including those that cause ringworm, thereby aiding in its treatment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, which possess antifungal and antimicrobial properties effective against ringworm.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and fight fungal infections, making it a useful addition to tinctures for treating ringworm.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus contain oils that have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment and prevention of ringworm infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and dry them in a clean dark area for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and combine them with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which target the causative agents of this common skin condition. The herb's main active compound, methyl salicylate, has been shown to inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal spores, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair. Additionally, wintergreen tincture's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects help soothe itching, redness, and scaling associated with ringworm, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms and supporting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Wintergreen tincture contains salicylic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause ringworm, and help to reduce symptoms such as itching and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wintergreen's salicylic acid also has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe itchy, irritated skin and reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm infections.

Antioxidant properties: Wintergreen tincture contains antioxidants that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate ringworm symptoms and slow down the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Phenolics (Gaultherin): Gaultherin, a phenolic compound found in Gaultheria procumbens, has antifungal properties that can help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for ringworm, such as Trichophyton species.

Terpenoids (Cadinene): Cadinene, a sesquiterpenoid present in Gaultheria procumbens, exhibits antifungal activity that can help combat fungal infections, including those caused by ringworm, by disrupting fungal cell membrane function and inhibiting fungal growth.

Tannins: Tannins, a class of polyphenolic compounds in Gaultheria procumbens, have been reported to possess antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the spread of ringworm by inhibiting fungal replication and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections such as ringworm.
Roots: They are used due to their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating skin infections like ringworm.
Barks: They are used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the morning when dew is present.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried gaultheria procumbens with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this skin condition. The alpha acids in hops have been shown to inhibit the growth and spread of fungal spores, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, hops tincture's antimicrobial properties help to destroy any bacteria or other microorganisms that may be contributing to the infection. remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Hops tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, including those that cause ringworm, due to its ability to disrupt fungal cell membranes and prevent the formation of biofilms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hops tincture contains compounds like humulone and lupulone, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

remedy_constituents: Lupulone: This sesquiterpene has antifungal properties, which may help combat fungal infections such as ringworm by inhibiting the growth of the causative agent.
Humulone: Another sesquiterpene found in Humulus lupulus, humulone has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which may aid in controlling the spread of ringworm and promoting healing.

Xanthohumol: A prenylated chalcone present in Humulus lupulus, xanthohumol has been reported to possess antifungal and antioxidant properties, which could help mitigate the effects of ringworm by reducing oxidative stress and fungal proliferation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: They are used for their flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: They are used due to their flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried humulus lupulus cones and add them to a clean glass jar with 2 parts of vodka or ethanol at 70% proof.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion to occur slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid tincture to a dropper bottle with a tight-fitting lid for storage.
Label the bottle with the date and contents and store it in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 parts of vodka by volume in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and add 1 part of glycerin to the liquid, mixing well to combine.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree tinctures helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which allow it to effectively target and combat the fungal infection. The tannins and terpenoids present in tea tree oil have been shown to inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi, making it an ideal natural remedy for ringworm treatment. remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh leaves from mature plants in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create a solvent mixture.
Steep the solvent mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the solvent mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

Clean and dry the ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 1 part ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

Chop the leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 100g of chopped pelargonium graveolens leaves with 500ml of vodka in a glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the jar in a dark place for 14 days allowing the mixture to steep and extract essential oils.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh rosa damascena flowers in the morning when they are dewy.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 250 grams of dried rosa damascena flowers with 1000 milliliters of pure grain vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain and filter the resulting tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba bark in spring or fall with clean scissors and store it in paper bags.
Clean and dry the harvested bark for at least 24 hours in a warm oven set at 150°f with no air circulation.
Chop the dried bark into small pieces using sharp knives or mortars and pestles for about 30 minutes.
Mix one part chopped bark with one part high-proof ethanol 95% in glass jars leaving no headspace for 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into dark glass bottles filtering out any sediment and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sassafras albidum bark in the early morning for optimal potency retention.
Dry the harvested bark using shade drying or air circulation methods to prevent mold growth.
Chop the dried bark into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka at a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Store the mixture in an amber glass jar, allowing it to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids, and transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

Additionally, thyme's antibacterial properties can help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a valuable natural treatment option for alleviating symptoms like itching, redness, and scaly patches associated with ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Thyme's antifungal compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to inhibit the growth of fungal infections like ringworm, preventing further spread and promoting healing.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: The tannins and phenolic acids present in thyme have antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent secondary infections, which are common complications of ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's flavonoids and terpenes possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm, reducing discomfort and promoting a more rapid recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are valued for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making them effective in treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality thymus vulgaris dried herb in large quantities approximately one ounce per batch.
Mix the dried thymus vulgaris herb with 80 proof vodka or rum at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for four weeks stirring daily for maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Store the finished thymus vulgaris tincture in a cool dark place protected from light and moisture for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which can effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as citral and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi, including those responsible for ringworm. Additionally, lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing and treating this condition.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and promoting healthy skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena tincture reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery process.
Antifungal properties: Lemon verbena's antifungal properties directly target the fungal agents that cause ringworm, helping to clear up infections and prevent recurrence.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antifungal compounds, roots are effective against fungal infections like ringworm.
Seeds: Seeds contain oil with antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal and bacterial infections associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems, wash them thoroughly in cold water and pat dry.
Chop the herbs finely to ensure optimal extraction of their active compounds and antimicrobial properties.
Combine one part chopped herb with two parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, store at room temperature for 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard solids and label the liquid.
Store the finished tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet tinctures helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The herb's active compounds, such as acetoside and gallic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including Candida and Malassezia species, which are commonly associated with ringworm. Additionally, sweet violet tinctures may help soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation, promoting a healthy environment for wound healing.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Sweet violet tincture contains antifungal compounds like flavonoids and saponins that inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Trichophyton rubrum, which causes ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and glycosides present in sweet violet tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Wound-healing promotion: Sweet violet's antioxidant and antibacterial compounds can promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing the risk of secondary infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, sweet violet roots have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective against fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhizomes: As the underground stem system of the plant, rhizomes contain a high concentration of essential oils that can help combat fungal growth and alleviate ringworm symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viola odorata leaves and stems at peak potency during early morning hours.
Cleanse the plant material by gently rinsing with cool water and pat drying with paper towels.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 60% vodka or rum in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily for 20-30 seconds each time.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which allow it to effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The antibacterial compounds present in ginger also help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief from discomfort. Additionally, the antiviral properties of ginger tincture may help prevent secondary infections that can occur when the skin is compromised by the ringworm fungus.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Ginger's antifungal compound, gingerol, inhibits the growth of fungal pathogens, including those responsible for ringworm, thereby preventing infection and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, reduce inflammation and irritation caused by ringworm, alleviating symptoms like redness, itching, and swelling.
Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate ringworm and slow down healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the ginger plant have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Roots: The roots of ginger contain compounds that exhibit antifungal activity, which can help combat ringworm infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots carefully selecting those with no signs of pests or diseases.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly under cold running water and dry them with clean towels.
Chop the dried ginger roots into small pieces and combine with 80% vodka in a ratio of one part ginger to two parts liquid.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks in a dark glass jar stored in a cool place shaking occasionally to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids storing it in a cool dark place for future use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for ringworm are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs used to treat fungal infections, such as ringworm. These tinctures work by killing fungi that cause ringworm, soothing itchy skin, and promoting healthy skin regeneration. Examples of effective herbal tinctures for ringworm include tea tree oil, lavender oil, and calendula extract. By using these tinctures, individuals can experience relief from the discomfort and embarrassment caused by ringworm, allowing them to live a more confident and comfortable life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal tinctures are skin conditions such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and eczema. Herbal tinctures like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and calendula extract have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal infected areas. These tinctures can be applied topically or taken internally in capsule form to support the body's natural healing process.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with ringworm is a blend of Tea Tree (Melaleuca alternifolia), Calendula, and Echinacea. Tea Tree tincture's antimicrobial properties combat fungal infections, while Calendula soothes and calms the skin. Echinacea enhances the immune system, aiding in the body's natural healing process. A 1:1:1 ratio of these tinctures can be mixed and applied topically to the affected area 2-3 times a day, or taken orally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
